intro:
A strong password generator creates random, secure passwords that are virtually impossible for hackers to guess or crack using brute-force attacks. Using weak or reused passwords is one of the leading causes of account breaches, identity theft, and data leaks. Our free password generator creates cryptographically random passwords of any length with your choice of character sets.

Security experts recommend using unique passwords of at least 16 characters for every online account, combining uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special symbols. Remembering dozens of such passwords is impossible without a password manager — which is why generating strong random passwords and storing them securely is the modern standard for digital security.

Whether you need a password for a bank account, email, social media, or any other service, our generator creates passwords that meet the strictest security requirements. Generate as many as you need, completely free, with no data stored or transmitted.
howItWorks: Select your desired password length and choose which character types to include: uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and special symbols. Click Generate and a cryptographically random password is created instantly. Click the copy button to copy it to your clipboard, then paste it into your account registration or password manager.
interpretationGuide: Longer passwords with more character variety are exponentially harder to crack. A 16-character password using all character types has more possible combinations than there are atoms in the observable universe, making it effectively uncrackable by brute force. Use a password manager to store generated passwords securely.

faqs.question: How long should a secure password be?
faqs.answer: Security experts recommend at least 12-16 characters for most accounts, and 20+ characters for highly sensitive accounts like banking and email. Longer passwords are exponentially harder to crack even with advanced hardware.

faqs.question: Should I use special characters in passwords?
faqs.answer: Yes, including special characters (!@#$%^&*) significantly increases password strength by expanding the character pool from 62 to 95 characters, increasing the number of possible combinations by orders of magnitude.

faqs.question: Is it safe to use an online password generator?
faqs.answer: Our generator creates passwords entirely in your browser using JavaScript — no password is ever sent to a server or stored anywhere. The generation uses your browser's cryptographically secure random number generator.

faqs.question: Should I use the same password for multiple sites?
faqs.answer: Never reuse passwords. If one site is breached, attackers use credential stuffing to try your password on other sites. Use a unique generated password for every account, stored in a password manager.

faqs.question: What is a password manager?
faqs.answer: A password manager is software that securely stores all your passwords encrypted behind a single master password. Popular options include Bitwarden (free), 1Password, and Dashlane. They can autofill passwords on websites.

faqs.question: What makes a password weak?
faqs.answer: Weak passwords include dictionary words, names, birth dates, sequential numbers (123456), keyboard patterns (qwerty), or anything personally identifiable. Short passwords under 8 characters are also very weak.

faqs.question: How often should I change my passwords?
faqs.answer: Change passwords immediately if you suspect a breach, if a site notifies you of a data leak, or if you shared a password with someone you no longer trust. Regular arbitrary rotation is less important than using unique strong passwords.

faqs.question: Can I use passphrases instead of random passwords?
faqs.answer: Passphrases (four or more random words like correct-horse-battery-staple) are memorable and strong. However, random character passwords of the same length are slightly stronger. Use whichever your account accepts.

faqs.question: What is two-factor authentication?
faqs.answer: Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds a second verification step after your password, such as a code from an authenticator app. Even if your password is stolen, attackers cannot access your account without the second factor.

faqs.question: Are generated passwords truly random?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our generator uses the Web Crypto API (window.crypto.getRandomValues), which provides cryptographically secure randomness — the same standard used in security software and encryption tools.
